label: In the Beginning
decision: God creates the heavens and the earth
immediate_fruit: The cosmos exists — formless, empty, dark, with the Spirit hovering over the waters
long_term_fruit: Every subsequent decision has a stage to act upon

children.label: Day 1
children.decision: Let there be light
children.immediate_fruit: Light exists; God sees that it is good; light and darkness separated
children.long_term_fruit: Vision, photosynthesis, the rhythm of day and night, the measurement of time

children.label: Light → Day / Darkness → Night
children.decision: God names the light Day and the darkness Night
children.immediate_fruit: Language enters creation — naming as an act of sovereignty
children.long_term_fruit: The pattern of naming-as-dominion; Adam will be invited to continue it

children.label: Day 2
children.decision: Let there be an expanse to separate the waters above from the waters below
children.immediate_fruit: The atmosphere exists; sky and sea separated
children.long_term_fruit: Weather, clouds, rain, the water cycle — all life's hydration depends on this separation

children.label: Expanse → Heaven
children.decision: God names the expanse Heaven
children.immediate_fruit: The sky is given identity
children.long_term_fruit: Heaven as a concept — both physical and theological; the dwelling place language begins here

children.label: Day 3
children.decision: Let the waters be gathered; let dry land appear
children.immediate_fruit: Seas and dry earth exist; God sees that it is good
children.long_term_fruit: The foundation for every land creature, every civilization, every city

children.label: Dry land → Earth / Waters → Seas
children.decision: God names the dry land Earth and the gathered waters Seas
children.immediate_fruit: Geography is named into existence
children.long_term_fruit: The named world becomes navigable, mappable, habitable

children.label: Let the earth bring forth vegetation
children.decision: Let the earth sprout vegetation — plants yielding seed, trees bearing fruit
children.immediate_fruit: All plant life exists; God sees that it is good
children.long_term_fruit: Food, medicine, oxygen, wood, fiber — the biological foundation of every human civilization

children.label: Day 4
children.decision: Let there be lights in the expanse to separate day from night; signs for seasons, days, years
children.immediate_fruit: Sun, moon, and stars exist; God sees that it is good
children.long_term_fruit: Agriculture (planting seasons), navigation, calendars, worship calendars, the Sabbath cycle

children.label: Lights to govern day and night
children.decision: God makes two great lights — the greater to govern the day, the lesser to govern the night — and the stars
children.immediate_fruit: The sun and moon are given governance roles — the first delegated authority in creation
children.long_term_fruit: The pattern of delegated rule that will extend to humanity in Day 6

children.label: Day 5
children.decision: Let the waters swarm with living creatures; let birds fly above the earth
children.immediate_fruit: Sea creatures and birds exist — every living creature that moves; God sees that it is good
children.long_term_fruit: Animal life; the ocean ecosystem; birds as pollinators and seed-spreaders

children.label: First Blessing: Be fruitful and multiply
children.decision: God blesses them: Be fruitful and multiply, fill the waters and the earth
children.immediate_fruit: The first blessing in Scripture — authority and commission given to creatures
children.long_term_fruit: Biological multiplication; the pattern of blessing-as-commissioning that will reach humanity and Abraham

children.label: Day 6
children.decision: Let the earth bring forth living creatures — livestock, creeping things, beasts of the earth
children.immediate_fruit: Land animals exist; God sees that it is good
children.long_term_fruit: Domestication, agriculture, the animal kingdom as context for human civilization

children.label: Let us make man in our image
children.decision: God decides to make mankind in His image and likeness, to have dominion over every living thing
children.immediate_fruit: Humanity exists — male and female; God sees that it is very good
children.long_term_fruit: Every human being who has ever lived; language, culture, civilization, redemption history

children.label: Second Blessing + Dominion Commission
children.decision: God blesses them: Be fruitful and multiply, fill the earth, subdue it, have dominion
children.immediate_fruit: Humanity is commissioned — not just blessed to multiply but given authority to steward creation
children.long_term_fruit: All of human history; stewardship and dominion become the framework for every human decision that follows — and the authority Adam fails to exercise: the serpent is a creature over which Adam had already been given dominion (Gen 1:28); his silence in Genesis 3 is not merely disobedience, it is an abdication of authority he already held

children.label: Every plant is given for food
children.decision: God gives every seed-bearing plant and fruit tree to mankind and every green plant to animals
children.immediate_fruit: Provision established — no scarcity in the original design
children.long_term_fruit: The original diet; the context against which the forbidden tree will be placed; abundance as the baseline

children.label: God forms man from dust, breathes life
children.decision: God forms man from the dust of the ground and breathes into his nostrils the breath of life
children.immediate_fruit: Adam becomes a living soul — a being of both dust and divine breath; the first creature animated by God's direct breath
children.long_term_fruit: The tension between dust-nature and Spirit-breath runs through all of Scripture; resurrection restores both; 'dust you are and to dust you shall return' (Gen 3:19) is the reversal of this act

children.label: God plants Eden; places man in it
children.decision: God plants a garden in Eden and places the man He formed in it
children.immediate_fruit: Man has a home prepared for him — a place of beauty and provision before he can ask for one
children.long_term_fruit: Eden as the original template for the Promised Land, the Tabernacle, the Temple, the New Jerusalem; garden as sacred meeting-place throughout Scripture

children.label: Tree of Life + Tree of Knowledge planted
children.decision: God makes every beautiful and edible tree grow in the garden, including the Tree of Life and the Tree of the Knowledge of Good and Evil
children.immediate_fruit: The garden is a place of abundance; but two special trees introduce meaning, choice, and consequence into the landscape
children.long_term_fruit: The Tree of Life reappears in Revelation 22 — its access restored to those who overcome; the Tree of Knowledge becomes the site of the first test of trust

children.label: Man placed to work and keep the garden
children.decision: God places man in the garden with a vocation: to work it and keep it
children.immediate_fruit: Work is established as holy before the fall — man has dignity and purpose through labor, not merely through existence
children.long_term_fruit: The theology of vocation; work as worship; stewardship as the human calling; the fall will curse the ground but not abolish work — labor remains, now with friction

children.label: First Command: Do not eat the tree of knowledge
children.decision: God commands man: every tree of the garden is freely given, but the Tree of the Knowledge of Good and Evil must not be eaten
children.immediate_fruit: A boundary is established within abundance — man has freedom and full provision, with one restraint that is also an act of trust
children.long_term_fruit: The foundational structure of all covenant: command + promise for obedience + consequence for disobedience; the Mosaic covenant follows this same architecture; the New Covenant resolves what this moment breaks

children.label: Promise: every tree freely given
children.decision: God grants free access to every tree of the garden — including the Tree of Life — as the provision for living within the covenant boundary
children.immediate_fruit: Abundance is the baseline; the restriction is one tree out of every tree; obedience preserves ongoing access to life itself
children.long_term_fruit: The Tree of Life access is closed after the fall (Gen 3:24); restoration of that access becomes the eschatological promise (Revelation 22:2, 14)

children.label: Warning: you shall surely die
children.decision: God declares the consequence for eating the forbidden tree: 'in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die'
children.immediate_fruit: Death is named as a real possibility — something not part of the original creation now stands as the consequence of broken trust
children.long_term_fruit: The death that follows in Genesis 3 is both immediate (spiritual separation from God) and eventual (physical); Paul's argument in Romans 5–6 rests entirely on this moment; 'the wages of sin is death'

children.label: It is not good for man to be alone
children.decision: God declares that man's aloneness is 'not good' and decides to make a suitable helper for him
children.immediate_fruit: The first time God names something 'not good' — incompleteness is recognized and the decision to remedy it is made before the remedy exists
children.long_term_fruit: The theology of human community; marriage as God's design for completeness; the church as the community that mirrors this; Christ who will not leave His people as orphans

children.label: God brings animals to Adam to name
children.decision: God forms animals and brings them to Adam to see what he would call them — and whatever Adam called each creature, that was its name
children.immediate_fruit: God creates deliberate space for human agency and ratifies the result: Adam's naming is honored
children.long_term_fruit: Language as a distinctly human vocation; taxonomy and natural science as extensions of this act; the pattern of God-initiates, human-responds throughout covenant history

children.label: Adam names all creatures
children.decision: Adam names every living creature, exercising the stewardship mandate — and finds that none of them is a suitable partner for him
children.immediate_fruit: Every animal has a name; Adam's dominion over creation is expressed through language; his aloneness is confirmed more acutely by the search itself
children.long_term_fruit: The first human decision God explicitly honors in Scripture; naming as an act of knowing and relating; the theology of language as a distinctly human gift rooted in the image of God

children.label: God builds woman from Adam's side
children.decision: God causes Adam to sleep, takes from his side, and builds the woman — then brings her to Adam
children.immediate_fruit: Woman exists — not formed from dust separately but from Adam's own body; 'built' (Hebrew: bnh — the word used for temples and cities) rather than merely formed
children.long_term_fruit: The indissoluble unity of man and woman; 'bone of my bones'; Ephesians 5 (the marriage mystery pointing to Christ and His church); Christ's side pierced at the cross — from which His bride is built

children.label: Adam names and receives the woman
children.decision: Adam recognizes the woman as 'bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh' and names her Woman — because she was taken from Man
children.immediate_fruit: The first recorded human speech in Scripture — and it is a covenant declaration of recognition, not a command or complaint
children.long_term_fruit: Marriage covenant as mutual recognition and naming; Adam's faithfulness here is the foil for his silence in Genesis 3; the second Adam (Christ) will not remain silent when His bride is threatened

children.label: God's decree: leave, cleave, one flesh
children.decision: God declares the institution of marriage: a man shall leave his father and mother, cleave to his wife, and they shall become one flesh
children.immediate_fruit: Marriage is established as the covenant of leaving and uniting — a new primary loyalty formed from the original unity
children.long_term_fruit: Every marriage in human history; Jesus quotes this in Matthew 19 when challenged on divorce; Paul calls it a mystery pointing to Christ and the church (Ephesians 5:31–32)

children.label: Day 7
children.decision: God chooses to rest on the seventh day from all His work
children.immediate_fruit: Rest enters creation — not because God was exhausted (Isaiah 40:28 rules that out), but as a sovereign act of completion: work finished, a boundary drawn
children.long_term_fruit: The six-plus-one rhythm woven into time itself; the category of rest as something distinct from sleep or inactivity — a chosen cessation

children.label: Seventh Day Blessed and Sanctified
children.decision: God blesses the seventh day and sanctifies it — sets it apart as holy because He rested on it
children.immediate_fruit: The first holy TIME in Scripture; holiness extends beyond objects and places into the fabric of time itself
children.long_term_fruit: The Sabbath commandment (Exodus 20:8–11 explicitly grounds it here); the sabbatical year (Leviticus 25); the Year of Jubilee; Hebrews 4 (the rest that remains for the people of God); the eschatological Sabbath rest
